title: Transhumanist themes in NGE
wikitext:
[]: Transhumanist themes in NGE             Quote Post by EarthScorpion » Fri Oct 16, 2009 2:19 pm Themaninblack wrote: Part of the reasons why I love it. Though, I'd consider it ANTI-transhumanist due to its bleakness. Transhumanist means things will get better for us furless apes and that while the universe is indifferent to us, there is a place for us in it after all. Your fan fic...not so much really.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:11 pm Themaninblack wrote: EarthScorpion wrote: I'm using Cthulhutech as the base, which means that it's closer to pure Lovecraft, and is also sort of proto-transhumanist bleakdepressing sci-fi.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:13 pm Mr. Tines wrote: For a series that flirts with transhumanist (or at the very least, post-humanist) themes, depressingly little of the fan-base seems to have clued into this -- so many "fix up the teen romance" fics, so little playing with the SFnal themes underpinning the series; whereas the first thing I vomited forth after watching the series was pure Transcension.

[]:             Quote Post by EarthScorpion » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:15 pm Lucretius wrote: But does it really deal with Trnahumanism in a positive way? The giant rotting Rei lingers in the mind longer than the "Congratulations" scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:36 pm EarthScorpion wrote: Why must Transhumanism be nice?

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:45 pm This. NGE's about the characters more than the half-assedly examined sci-fi themes anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:56 pm Lucretius wrote: But does it really deal with Trnahumanism in a positive way? The giant rotting Rei lingers in the mind longer than the "Congratulations" scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:06 pm Lucretius wrote: This. NGE's about the characters more than the half-assedly examined sci-fi themes anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:08 pm Shouldn't that be in a spoiler tag?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:08 pm what the heck is this thread about? Some wacky nasty lovecrafitian sf-book or NGE? *confused* Also, transhumanism sucks. A modern paradise and utopy born out of our superficial, "modern" and "rational" fantasies. As good indicator of humanity's pitiful grasping at earthly immortality as there has ever been. Pointless twitching against some cosmical demiurge. In the end entropy is going to get all the fuckers and slurp all the information. And all that remains is neverending darkness. Happy end? but don't mind me! I am just the silly transcendentalist poet from 19th century!

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:09 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Shouldn't that be in a spoiler tag?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:15 pm I have only interest in the Pizza out of those two.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:21 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I have only interest in the Pizza out of those two.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Oct 16, 2009 4:36 pm I love you, Eric.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Fri Aug 27, 2010 4:49 am EarthScorpion wrote: Why must Transhumanism be nice ? It simply needs to be.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 27, 2010 2:33 pm As long as this thread is being necro'd. Xard wrote: Also, transhumanism sucks. A modern paradise and utopy born out of our superficial, "modern" and "rational" fantasies. As good indicator of humanity's pitiful grasping at earthly immortality as there has ever been. Pointless twitching against some cosmical demiurge. In the end entropy is going to get all the fuckers and slurp all the information. And all that remains is neverending darkness. Happy end?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Aug 27, 2010 3:26 pm AuraTwilight wrote: By this reasoning, 19th century transcendentalist poetry sucks. It's just pointless twitching because in the end entropy is gonna get all you fuckers and slurp up all the information.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 28, 2010 3:25 pm Yes. There's no meaning or value to anything you do, see, know or experience. Transcendentalist poetry is no exception to the rule

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Aug 28, 2010 5:17 pm AuraTwilight wrote: So then what makes Transhumanism more silly/less worthwhile than the poetry you like? Because you do seem to speak with a tone of superiority.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 28, 2010 11:33 pm Sure is getting pretty technophobic up in here for a bunch of folks talking on the internet... Xard wrote: As such vast majority of the more "out there" theorizing of transhumanists are little more than absurd fantasies.
summary:
[]: Transhumanist themes in NGE             Quote Post by EarthScorpion » Fri Oct 16, 2009 2:19 pm Themaninblack wrote: Part of the reasons why I love it. Though, I'd consider it ANTI-transhumanist due to its bleakness. Transhumanist means things will get better for us furless apes and that while the universe is indifferent to us, there is a place for us in it after all. Your fan fic...not so much really.

[]:     &n
